← the Atlas

The active-tile indicator — a channel apart from agent status

feature · seedling ·implemented ·JUN 13, 2026

Now that the tile border is owned by the animated agent-status ring (+ repo identity), the focused-tile indicator must move off the border to a completely separate channel. The current teal right-edge competes with the ring; here are live prototypes of where focus should live instead — chrome, depth, or an outer glow.

Contents

  • Today — and why it’s out
  • The options
    • A — Accent title bar
    • B — Lift / elevation
    • C — Outer accent glow
  • Recommendation

The canvas tile now carries an animated agent-status ring on its border (marching ants / sweeping comet, in the terminal’s theme colour — see canvas-tile-state-borders). That ring, plus the repo-identity border colour, means the border is spoken for. But the active / focused tile is still marked the old way — a teal --color-accent right-edge + a 1px accent ring — which now sits right on top of the status ring and reads as a second, competing border.

The ask

The active indicator should be completely different from the agent-status thing — its own channel, not another coloured border. (And the teal right-edge has to go.)

Every tile below wears its agent-status comet (the spinning border light). The middle tile is the focused one. The question is only how focus is marked.

Today — and why it’s out

rejected The focused tile gets a teal accent on its right edge + a 1px accent ring. Sitting on the same border the status comet travels, it reads as a second border fighting the first — exactly the clash you flagged.

Today · teal right-edge + accent ring (competes with the status ring)
blog · codex
◐ working
argh · claude
⏵ waiting
odu · claude
◐ working

The options

All three move focus off the border entirely, so the border belongs to repo identity + agent status alone.

A — Accent title bar

Recommended The focused tile’s title bar lights up in the accent (a tint + a thin accent underline). Focus lives in the chrome; the border is never touched. Unmistakable, and it can’t be confused with the status ring because it’s a different part of the tile. Bonus: the title bar is where you read the tile’s identity anyway, so the eye is already there.

A · accent title bar — focus in the chrome (recommended)
blog · codex
◐ working
argh · claude
⏵ waiting
odu · claude
◐ working

B — Lift / elevation

No colour at all: the focused tile raises (a stronger drop shadow) and the others recede (dim). Pure depth — nothing can compete with the border because focus isn’t a border treatment at all. Calmest and most physical, but more subtle across a busy canvas, and it leans on the inactive tiles dimming to read.

B · lift + recede — depth only, no colour
blog · codex
◐ working
argh · claude
⏵ waiting
odu · claude
◐ working

C — Outer accent glow

A soft accent halo outside the tile (plus a faint lift). Different geometry from the on-border comet (it bleeds outward, not on the edge), so it doesn’t overlap the ring — but it still puts the accent colour near the border, so on a dense canvas it can read as busy next to the comet. The middle ground.

C · outer accent glow — accent, but off the edge
blog · codex
◐ working
argh · claude
⏵ waiting
odu · claude
◐ working

Recommendation

A — accent title bar, optionally with a hint of B

A is the cleanest separation: the border = repo identity + agent status; the title bar = focus. Two different surfaces, zero overlap, and the accent colour never appears on the border. Pair it with a gentle version of B (the focused tile lifts a touch, inactive tiles dim to ~0.92 as they already do) for depth, and drop the teal right-edge and the accent ring from packages/client/src/canvas/CanvasTile.tsx entirely. C is the fallback if you want the accent somewhere near the tile, but it’s the most likely to read busy beside the comet.

Chosen: A — accent title bar

Shipped in #1335 . packages/client/src/canvas/CanvasTile.tsx drops the teal border-right-color and the 0 0 0 1px var(--color-accent) box-shadow ring; the focused tile’s title bar now tints --color-accent (a 22% mix over the theme bg) with an accent underline. The active tile keeps its existing lift (deeper drop shadow) for depth — the “hint of B”. The border is left to repo identity + the agent-status ring.

Status: implemented in #1335 — option A (accent title bar); a follow-on to the agent-status ring (canvas-tile-state-borders).
